task_completion.description: Open the Copper Point of Sale application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Copper Point of Sale main application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the operator or staff management section
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog, window, or tab specifically for managing operators, staff, or users is open.

task_completion.description: Enter the name for the first operator
task_completion.visual_evidence: The name 'Emily Rodriguez' is typed into the name field of the new operator creation form.

task_completion.description: Enter the name for the second operator
task_completion.visual_evidence: The name 'James Nakamura' is typed into the name field of the new operator creation form.

task_completion.description: Verify both accounts are saved and appear in the operator list
task_completion.visual_evidence: Both 'Emily Rodriguez' and 'James Nakamura' are clearly visible as saved entries in the operator list or table.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to create the accounts
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Copper POS GUI forms and buttons, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not directly modify the database file
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open showing SQLite commands or scripts modifying 'copper.db' directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Copper POS software
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window shows genuine Copper Point of Sale branding (by NCH Software), not a text editor or mock interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Software: Copper Point of Sale (by NCH Software, version 3.06). Operator 1 Name: Emily Rodriguez. Operator 2 Name: James Nakamura. The operator data is stored in the Copper database (copper.db), but the VLM should verify the successful creation of the accounts through the Copper POS GUI operator list.
